title: RCausalModel
text: R package port Python causal inference library implementing estimators observational studies, randomized experiments, network interference settings. Based Qu et al. (2021). Python reference implementation.

title: RCausalModel
text: R/ — package source (interference.R, random_data.R, observational.R, etc.) tests/testthat/ — 113 tests including slow simulation tests vignettes/ — quickstart.Rmd (usage walkthrough), simulations.Rmd (Monte Carlo validation), observational.Rmd (mirrors Python observational.ipynb), experimental.Rmd (mirrors Python experimental.ipynb), interference.Rmd (mirrors Python Interference.ipynb) Parent project Python source: ~/projects/claude/CausalModel

title: RCausalModel
code: Rscript -e 'devtools::test()' Rscript -e 'devtools::check(cran=FALSE)' Rscript -e 'devtools::document()' Rscript -e 'devtools::build_vignettes()'

title: RCausalModel
text: simulation tests (normality, coverage) slow (10+ minutes) — skipped skip_on_cran() used slow tests run locally estimators: OLS, IPW, AIPW, matching, DML, difference--means, strata, ANCOVA, Fisher test, clustered IPW/AIPW

title: RCausalModel
text: S3 classes (idiomatic R) rather R6/R5 Model wrappers use closure-based protocol: list fit(X, y) predict(model, X) functions HC0 robust SEs computed manually avoid sandwich dependency RANN::nn2() replaces scipy.spatial.KDTree matching Dependencies: stats (base), RANN (matching), nnet (multinomial logistic)

title: RCausalModel
text: R column-major: flattening 3D arrays (clusters, nunit, ngroup) 2D matrices, use aperm(arr, c(2, 1, 3)) matrix() match Python’s row-major reshape behavior Python reference implementation — R results diverge, R code likely wrong Watch R/Python differences: column-major vs row-major, 1-indexing vs 0-indexing

title: RCausalModel
text: interference.R get_final_tuple aperm fix (2026-03-17): augmented covariate matrix X_aug (3D: clusters × nunit × k_aug) flattened 2D via matrix(X_aug, ...) without aperm(), scrambling covariate rows relative Y, Z, G. Fix: matrix(aperm(X_aug, c(2,1,3)), ...). column-major reshape issue documented Gotchas — G computation already fix X . Impact: AIPW coverage rare g values went ~60% ~94%.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Zhaonan Qu. Author, maintainer. CausalModel Contributors. Contributor.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Qu Z (2026). CausalModel: Causal Inference Estimators Observational Experimental Studies. R package version 0.1.0, https://xiangao.github.io/RCausalModel/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {CausalModel: Causal Inference Estimators for Observational and Experimental Studies},   author = {Zhaonan Qu},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://xiangao.github.io/RCausalModel/}, }

what: Causal Inference Estimators for Observational and Experimental Studies
title: Causal Inference Estimators for Observational and Experimental Studies
text: R package causal inference estimation observational studies, randomized experiments, network interference settings. Port Python CausalModel package (Qu et al. 2021).

title: Causal Inference Estimators for Observational and Experimental Studies
code: # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("username/RCausalModel")

title: Causal Inference Estimators for Observational and Experimental Studies
code: library(CausalModel)  # Generate synthetic data with known ATE = 10 data <- generate_data(N = 5000, k = 2, tau = 10) obs <- observational(data$Y, data$Z, data$X)  # Estimators est_via_ols(obs)        # OLS with HC0 robust SE est_via_ipw(obs)        # Inverse probability weighting est_via_aipw(obs)       # Augmented IPW (doubly robust) est_via_matching(obs)   # Nearest-neighbor matching estimate(obs)           # Default (AIPW for binary Z)  # Continuous treatment -> Double ML data_c <- generate_data_continuous(N = 5000, k = 2, tau = 10) obs_c <- observational(data_c$Y, data_c$Z, data_c$X) est_via_dml(obs_c)

title: Causal Inference Estimators for Observational and Experimental Studies
code: exp_obj <- experimental(Y, Z, X)  est_via_dm(exp_obj)                    # Difference in means est_via_strata(exp_obj, strata = s)    # Stratified estimator est_via_ancova(exp_obj)                # ANCOVA with interactions test_via_fisher(exp_obj, n_draws = 1000)  # Fisher randomization test

title: Causal Inference Estimators for Observational and Experimental Studies
code: design <- crd(treated_ratio = 0.5)        # Completely randomized design <- bernoulli(treated_prob = 0.5)   # Bernoulli Z <- draw(design, n = 100)               # Draw treatment vector  # Rerandomization for covariate balance design <- crd(treated_ratio = 0.5, covariate = X, balance = TRUE, eps = 0.1)

title: Causal Inference Estimators for Observational and Experimental Studies
code: # Generate clustered data with spillover effects data <- generate_fixed_cluster(clusters = 5000, group_struct = c(2, 3, 4),                                 k = 2, tau = 1, gamma = c(0.5, 0.1, 0.2))  cl <- clustered(data$Y, data$Z, data$X,                 data$cluster_labels, data$group_labels,                 data$ingroup_labels)  # Estimate treatment effects under interference result <- est_via_ipw(cl)    # IPW under partial interference result <- est_via_aipw(cl)   # AIPW under partial interference

title: Causal Inference Estimators for Observational and Experimental Studies
text: Supply outcome/propensity models:
code: # Built-in wrappers est_via_aipw(obs, outcome_model = cm_ols(), propensity_model = cm_logistic())  # Random forest (requires randomForest package) est_via_aipw(obs,   outcome_model = cm_random_forest_regressor(),   propensity_model = cm_random_forest_classifier())

title: Causal Inference Estimators for Observational and Experimental Studies
text: Qu, Z., McNamara, R., Jia, R., & Imbens, G. (2021). CausalModel: Python Package Causal Inference.

what: Bind 3D arrays along third dimension — abind_3d
title: Bind 3D arrays along third dimension — abind_3d
text: Bind 3D arrays along third dimension

title: Bind 3D arrays along third dimension — abind_3d
code: abind_3d(a, b)

title: Bernoulli Randomized Design — bernoulli
text: Creates Bernoulli design unit independently assigned treatment probability treated_prob.

title: Bernoulli Randomized Design — bernoulli
code: bernoulli(   treated_prob = 0.5,   covariate = NULL,   balance = FALSE,   eps = 0.1,   max_iter = 1000 )

title: Bernoulli Randomized Design — bernoulli
text: treated_prob Numeric. Probability treatment. Default 0.5. covariate Numeric matrix. Covariates balance checking (optional). balance Logical. Use rerandomization. Default FALSE. eps Numeric. Balance criterion threshold. Default 0.1. max_iter Integer. Maximum rerandomization attempts. Default 1000.

title: Bernoulli Randomized Design — bernoulli
text: bernoulli design object.

what: Create a Clustered Interference Study Object — clustered
title: Create a Clustered Interference Study Object — clustered
text: Estimates causal effects partial/clustered interference, units within clusters may affect 's outcomes.

title: Create a Clustered Interference Study Object — clustered
code: clustered(   Y,   Z,   X,   cluster_labels,   group_labels,   ingroup_labels,   cluster_feature = NULL,   n_moments = 1L,   prop_idv_model = NULL,   prop_neigh_model = NULL,   n_matches = 100L,   subsampling_match = 2000L,   categorical_Z = TRUE )

title: Create a Clustered Interference Study Object — clustered
text: Y Numeric vector. Outcome variable. Z Numeric vector. Treatment indicator (0/1). X Numeric matrix. Individual-level covariates. cluster_labels Integer vector. Cluster identifier unit. group_labels Integer vector. Group index within cluster (0-indexed). ingroup_labels Integer vector. Position within group (0-indexed). cluster_feature Optional numeric matrix. Cluster-level covariates. n_moments Integer. Number moments covariate aggregation. Default 1. prop_idv_model Model wrapper individual propensity P(Z=1|X). prop_neigh_model Model wrapper neighborhood propensity P(G|X). n_matches Integer. Number matches variance estimation. Default 100. subsampling_match Integer. Maximum subsample size matching. Default 2000. categorical_Z Logical. Treat Z categorical. Default TRUE.

title: Create a Clustered Interference Study Object — clustered
text: clustered object.

what: Create a Causal Model Result — cm_result
title: Create a Causal Model Result — cm_result
text: Stores output causal inference estimator: point estimate, standard error, z-statistic, p-value, 95% confidence interval.

title: Create a Causal Model Result — cm_result
code: cm_result(ate, se, alpha = 0.05)

title: Create a Causal Model Result — cm_result
text: ate Numeric. Average treatment effect estimate. se Numeric. Standard error ATE. alpha Numeric. Significance level CI (default 0.05).

title: Create a Causal Model Result — cm_result
text: cm_result object (S3 list).

title: Completely Randomized Design — crd
text: Creates CRD design object random treatment assignment. Optionally supports rerandomization covariate balance.

title: Completely Randomized Design — crd
code: crd(   treated_ratio = 0.5,   covariate = NULL,   balance = FALSE,   eps = 0.1,   max_iter = 1000 )

title: Completely Randomized Design — crd
text: treated_ratio Numeric. Fraction units assigned treatment. Default 0.5. covariate Numeric matrix. Covariates balance checking (optional). balance Logical. Use rerandomization covariate balance. Default FALSE. eps Numeric. Balance criterion threshold. Default 0.1. max_iter Integer. Maximum rerandomization attempts. Default 1000.

title: Completely Randomized Design — crd
text: crd design object.

title: AIPW (Doubly Robust) Estimator — est_via_aipw
text: Augmented IPW estimator. Consistent either outcome model propensity model correctly specified.

title: AIPW (Doubly Robust) Estimator — est_via_aipw
code: est_via_aipw(obj, ...)  # S3 method for class 'observational' est_via_aipw(   obj,   outcome_model = NULL,   propensity_model = NULL,   treated_pred = NULL,   control_pred = NULL,   propensity = NULL,   ... )

title: AIPW (Doubly Robust) Estimator — est_via_aipw
text: obj observational clustered object. ... Additional arguments passed methods. outcome_model model wrapper fit predict. Default uses OLS. propensity_model model wrapper fit predict_proba. Default uses logistic regression. treated_pred Optional pre-computed treated outcome predictions. control_pred Optional pre-computed control outcome predictions. propensity Optional pre-computed propensity scores.

title: AIPW (Doubly Robust) Estimator — est_via_aipw
text: cm_result object.

title: ANCOVA Estimator — est_via_ancova
text: Fisher's ANCOVA treatment-covariate interactions HC0 robust standard errors. Regresses Y ~ 1 + Z + X + Z*X.

title: ANCOVA Estimator — est_via_ancova
code: est_via_ancova(obj, ...)  # S3 method for class 'experimental' est_via_ancova(obj, ...)

title: ANCOVA Estimator — est_via_ancova
text: obj experimental object. ... Additional arguments (currently unused).

title: ANCOVA Estimator — est_via_ancova
text: cm_result object.

title: Difference-in-Means Estimator — est_via_dm
text: Simple difference sample means treated control groups.

title: Difference-in-Means Estimator — est_via_dm
code: est_via_dm(obj, ...)  # S3 method for class 'experimental' est_via_dm(obj, ...)

title: Difference-in-Means Estimator — est_via_dm
text: obj experimental object. ... Additional arguments (currently unused).

title: Difference-in-Means Estimator — est_via_dm
text: cm_result object.

title: Double/Debiased Machine Learning Estimator — est_via_dml
text: DML estimator continuous treatments using cross-fitting.

title: Double/Debiased Machine Learning Estimator — est_via_dml
code: est_via_dml(obj, ...)  # S3 method for class 'observational' est_via_dml(   obj,   outcome_model = NULL,   treatment_model = NULL,   k_folds = 2,   ... )

title: Double/Debiased Machine Learning Estimator — est_via_dml
text: obj observational object. ... Additional arguments passed methods. outcome_model Model wrapper Y ~ X. Default uses OLS. treatment_model Model wrapper Z ~ X. Default uses OLS. k_folds Number cross-fitting folds. Default 2.

title: Double/Debiased Machine Learning Estimator — est_via_dml
text: cm_result object.

title: IPW Estimator — est_via_ipw
text: Inverse Probability Weighting estimator ATE.

title: IPW Estimator — est_via_ipw
code: est_via_ipw(obj, ...)  # S3 method for class 'observational' est_via_ipw(   obj,   propensity_model = NULL,   propensity = NULL,   normalize = TRUE,   ... )

title: IPW Estimator — est_via_ipw
text: obj observational clustered object. ... Additional arguments passed methods. propensity_model model wrapper fit predict_proba. Default uses logistic regression. propensity Optional numeric vector pre-estimated propensity scores. normalize Logical. Normalize weights (Hajek estimator). Default TRUE.

title: IPW Estimator — est_via_ipw
text: cm_result object.

title: Matching Estimator — est_via_matching
text: k-nearest-neighbor matching estimator ATE, optional bias adjustment.

title: Matching Estimator — est_via_matching
code: est_via_matching(obj, ...)  # S3 method for class 'observational' est_via_matching(   obj,   num_matches = 1,   num_matches_for_var = NULL,   bias_adj = FALSE,   ... )

title: Matching Estimator — est_via_matching
text: obj observational object. ... Additional arguments passed methods. num_matches Number matches per unit (M). Default 1. num_matches_for_var Number matches variance estimation (J). Default equals num_matches. bias_adj Logical. Apply bias correction using OLS. Default FALSE.

title: Matching Estimator — est_via_matching
text: cm_result object.

text: Estimates ATE via linear regression Y (Z, X) HC0 heteroskedasticity-robust standard errors.

code: est_via_ols(obj, ...)  # S3 method for class 'observational' est_via_ols(obj, ...)

title: OLS Estimator — est_via_ols
text: obj observational object. ... Additional arguments (currently unused).

title: OLS Estimator — est_via_ols
text: cm_result object.

title: Generate Clustered Data with Varying Cluster Sizes — generate_clustered_data
text: clusters_list Integer vector. Number clusters structure. group_struct_list List integer vectors. Group structures. tau Numeric. Direct treatment effect. Default 1. gamma Numeric vector. Spillover parameters.

text: list concatenated Y, Z, X, cluster_labels, group_labels, ingroup_labels, G, Xc.

title: Generate Observational Data — generate_data
text: Generates synthetic observational data (Y, Z, X) known treatment effect tau. propensity score sigmoid function covariates.

title: Generate Observational Data — generate_data
code: generate_data(N = 10000, k = 2, tau = 10)

title: Generate Observational Data — generate_data
text: N Integer. Number observations. Default 10000. k Integer. Number covariates. Default 2. tau Numeric. True average treatment effect. Default 10.

title: Generate Observational Data — generate_data
text: list components Y, Z, X.

title: Generate Observational Data — generate_data
code: data <- generate_data(N = 1000, k = 2, tau = 5) obs <- observational(data$Y, data$Z, data$X) est_via_aipw(obs) #> Causal Model Estimation Result #> ------------------------------ #>   ATE:      4.929815 #>   SE:       0.073875 #>   z:        66.7319 #>   p-value:  0.0000 #>   95% CI:   [4.785023, 5.074608]

what: Generate Fixed-Size Cluster Data with Interference — generate_fixed_cluster
title: Generate Fixed-Size Cluster Data with Interference — generate_fixed_cluster
text: Generates clustered data known direct effect tau spillover effects gamma.

title: Generate Fixed-Size Cluster Data with Interference — generate_fixed_cluster
code: generate_fixed_cluster(   clusters = 10000,   group_struct = c(2, 3, 4),   k = 2,   tau = 1,   gamma = NULL,   label_start = 0L )

title: Generate Fixed-Size Cluster Data with Interference — generate_fixed_cluster
text: clusters Integer. Number clusters. Default 10000. group_struct Integer vector. Number units per group within cluster. E.g., c(2, 3, 4) means 2 group 0, 3 group 1, 4 group 2. k Integer. Number individual covariates. Default 2. tau Numeric. Direct treatment effect. Default 1. gamma Numeric vector array. Spillover effect parameters. Default 0.1 group. label_start Integer. Starting cluster label. Default 0.

title: Generate Fixed-Size Cluster Data with Interference — generate_fixed_cluster
text: list components: Y, Z, X, cluster_labels, group_labels, ingroup_labels, G, Xc.

title: Compute Rerandomization Balance Criterion — get_balance
text: Mahalanobis-type balance criterion Morgan & Rubin (2015). $$m = \frac{n_t n_c}{n} (\bar{X}_t - \bar{X}_c)' \Sigma^{-1}   (\bar{X}_t - \bar{X}_c)$$

title: Compute Rerandomization Balance Criterion — get_balance
code: get_balance(Z, X)

title: Compute Rerandomization Balance Criterion — get_balance
text: Z Numeric vector. Treatment indicator. X Numeric matrix. Covariates.

title: Compute Rerandomization Balance Criterion — get_balance
text: Numeric scalar. Balance criterion value.

title: Fisher Randomization Test — test_via_fisher
text: Computes p-value comparing observed test statistic (DM) distribution random treatment reassignment.

title: Fisher Randomization Test — test_via_fisher
code: test_via_fisher(obj, ...)  # S3 method for class 'experimental' test_via_fisher(obj, n_draws = 1000, ...)

title: Fisher Randomization Test — test_via_fisher
text: obj experimental object. ... Additional arguments passed methods. n_draws Number random draws. Default 1000.

title: Fisher Randomization Test — test_via_fisher
text: Numeric p-value.
